overview.Summary: This scenario outlines a basic attack plan employed by Operation Spalax against communications companies.

attack_surface.AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Active Directory Scheduled Task Command

attack_vector.AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method Custom Command and Control Protocol Scheduled Task/Job Command Execution (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers use spear phishing via services to trick victims into divulging sensitive information.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit vulnerabilities in websites visited by employees to install malware on their devices.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attackers establish a connection between compromised systems and attacker-controlled servers to exfiltrate data.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers create scheduled tasks to run malicious code at regular intervals.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attackers transfer sensitive data from the compromised system to an external location.

Scenario.summary: Operation Spalax uses spear phishing and drive-by compromises to gain initial access, followed by custom command and control protocols, scheduled task execution, and automated exfiltration to achieve their objectives.
